name: Associated Credit Union of Texas
description_short: Openland Credit Union (formerly ACU of Texas) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering auto loans, mortgages, personal loans, and deposit accounts with competitive rates.
description_long:
Openland Credit Union is a federally-insured credit union that rebranded from its former name, Associated Credit Union of Texas (ACU of Texas). The organization operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving individuals seeking alternative banking with a community focus. The credit union has demonstrated financial health, having provided $5,923,116 in member savings last year, averaging $281 per household.

Openland offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including auto loans (as low as 4.24% APR for new and used vehicles), mortgage services (home purchase, refinance, home equity, and unimproved property), personal loans (as low as 9.29% APR unsecured), RV/boat/motorcycle loans (as low as 8.39% APR), and deposit products including Premier Checking with competitive interest rates. Members also have access to a MasterCard credit card product, educational savings plans, and bill payment services. The institution provides digital banking through a mobile app and online banking platform, with multiple branch and ATM locations available.

Openland distinguishes itself through personalized, local relationship banking emphasizing member service and community involvement. The company highlights availability through multiple contact channels (phone, text, email, in-person), fast approval processes for auto loans, and one-on-one support from local mortgage lenders. Their branding and messaging emphasize opening doors of opportunity and making space for what matters to members, positioning community commitment as a core differentiator.

As a credit union, Openland provides member-owned, not-for-profit banking with NCUA insurance protection. However, membership eligibility is restricted by field of membership requirements, which may limit accessibility compared to traditional banks. The website does not disclose specific APR ranges for mortgages, minimum credit score requirements, or detailed membership eligibility criteria, making it necessary to contact the institution directly for personalized rate quotes and qualification details.
services: Auto loans (new and used vehicles) with rates as low as 4.24% APR
Mortgage loans (purchase, refinance, home equity, unimproved property, second homes)
Personal unsecured loans as low as 9.29% APR
RV, boat, and motorcycle loans as low as 8.39% APR
Premier Checking account with competitive interest rates
College savings planning and accounts for educational expenses
Online banking platform with account access and bill payment
Mobile banking app with transaction history, transfers, and ATM/branch locator
Multiple branch locations and ATM network access
Member account enrollment and online login services
Payment processing from other financial institutions
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting at 4.24% APR for new and used vehicles
Comprehensive mortgage services including refinancing, home equity, and non-traditional property financing
Member savings totaling $5.9M last year ($281 average per household), demonstrating value
Multiple contact channels including phone, text, email, and branch visits for accessibility
Mobile app enabling account management, bill pay, transfers, and ATM/branch location finding
Local relationship banking with personal service emphasized in member testimonials
Unsecured personal loans available at rates as low as 9.29% APR
NCUA-insured deposits providing federal protection for member accounts
cons: Membership eligibility restricted by field of membership criteria, potentially excluding some consumers
Limited rate transparency on website; specific mortgage APR ranges not disclosed
Minimum credit score requirements and qualification criteria not clearly stated online
Geographic limitation to Texas service area based on original ACU of Texas foundation
Rebranding from ACU of Texas may cause confusion for existing members or those seeking the former entity
best_for: Texas residents and members eligible under Openland's field of membership seeking auto financing
Homebuyers and homeowners in Openland's service area wanting local mortgage lender relationships
Members prioritizing community involvement and credit union values over traditional banking
Consumers seeking competitive rates on auto loans and personal loans with relationship-based service
diagnosis: Openland Credit Union is best for Texas-based consumers eligible under its field of membership who value relationship banking, community involvement, and competitive rates on auto loans and mortgages. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted by eligibility criteria and geographic service area, and prospective members should contact the credit union directly to confirm qualification and obtain specific rate quotes, as detailed APR ranges for mortgages and minimum credit requirements are not fully disclosed online.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Associated Credit Union of Texas (League City, TX) |.
meta_description: Associated Credit Union of Texas — NCUA-insured credit union in League City, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
